req.title: Run payroll — variant 2
req.statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-073 defines a payroll-run transaction with five testable concerns: (1) happy-path completion with correct pay-stub and audit creation, (2) conflict rejection when a run is already in progress, (3) atomic rollback on internal failure, (4) idempotency under retry, and (5) boundary behaviour with exactly one active employee. EP covers the normal partition; NEG covers the conflict and failure partitions; BVA covers the single-employee boundary; STC covers the idle→running→completed state machine; DT is used for the idempotency retry scenario where the same request key re-enters a completed state.

tests.name: test_payroll_run_happy_path_completes_with_pay_stubs_and_audit
preconditions: caller has role hr-admin (valid JWT in <hr_admin_token> fixture)
at least two active employees exist with known salaries (<seed_active_employees> fixture)
no payroll run is currently in progress for the period
actions: poll or await until run state == completed (max 10 s via <wait_for_state> helper)
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with body.state in {queued, running, completed}
final run state == completed
pay-stub count equals number of seeded active employees
each pay-stub has gross_pay > 0, deductions >= 0, net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
audit log contains exactly one entry for the run
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_happy_path_completes_with_pay_stubs_and_audit(
    hr_admin_client, seed_active_employees, current_month_period, unique_idem_key
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        "/api/payroll-runs",
        json={"period": current_month_period},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": unique_idem_key},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]

    run = wait_for_state(hr_admin_client, f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}", "completed", timeout=10)
    assert run["state"] == "completed"

    stubs_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs")
    assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
    stubs = stubs_resp.json()["items"]
    assert len(stubs) == len(seed_active_employees)
    for stub in stubs:
        assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
        assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
        assert stub["net_pay"] == stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"]

    audit_resp = hr_admin_client.get(
        "/api/audit-log", params={"entity": "payroll-run", "entity_id": run_id}
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json()["items"]) >= 1

tests.name: test_payroll_run_returns_409_when_another_run_is_in_progress
preconditions: a payroll run for the same period is already in state=running (<lock_payroll_run_in_progress> fixture)
at least one active employee exists
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with period={current_month} and a fresh idempotency-key
inspect response status and error body
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict is returned
response body contains error code indicating a concurrent run exists
no new payroll-run record is created
trace: R-WEB-073: 409 if another run is in progress
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_returns_409_when_another_run_is_in_progress(
    hr_admin_client, lock_payroll_run_in_progress, current_month_period, unique_idem_key
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        "/api/payroll-runs",
        json={"period": current_month_period},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": unique_idem_key},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body

    list_resp = hr_admin_client.get(
        "/api/payroll-runs", params={"period": current_month_period, "state": "running"}
    )
    assert list_resp.status_code == 200
    runs = list_resp.json()["items"]
    # only the pre-existing locked run should be present
    assert len(runs) == 1
    assert runs[0]["id"] == lock_payroll_run_in_progress["id"]

tests.name: test_payroll_run_atomic_rollback_on_forced_failure
preconditions: fault-injection header or env flag causes the payroll engine to raise after partial processing (<fault_injection_flag> fixture)
multiple active employees seeded so partial commit is detectable
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with fault-injection header and a unique idempotency-key
query pay-stubs and audit log for the aborted run
expected: run reaches state=failed
HTTP response is 500 or run state reflects failure
zero pay-stub records exist for this run (no partial commit)
audit log records the failure event
trace: R-WEB-073: 500 rolled back atomically on failure
R-WEB-073: failed run shall not partially commit

tests.name: test_payroll_run_idempotent_on_retry_with_same_key
preconditions: a fixed idempotency-key is used for both requests
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with period={current_month} and idempotency-key=FIXED_KEY (first call)
POST /api/payroll-runs with identical payload and same idempotency-key=FIXED_KEY (retry)
expected: both POST calls return 2xx (201 on first, 200 or 201 on retry)
retry response body.id equals original run_id
pay-stub count is identical after retry (no duplicate stubs)
audit log has no duplicate completion entries caused by the retry
trace: R-WEB-073: action must be idempotent under retry

tests.name: test_payroll_run_boundary_single_active_employee
preconditions: exactly one active employee exists (<seed_single_employee> fixture); all others inactive
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with period={current_month} and unique idempotency-key
expected: run completes successfully (state=completed)
exactly one pay-stub is created
pay-stub net_pay == gross_pay - deductions for the single employee
inactive employees produce no pay-stub records
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_boundary_single_active_employee(
    hr_admin_client, seed_single_employee, current_month_period, unique_idem_key
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        "/api/payroll-runs",
        json={"period": current_month_period},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": unique_idem_key},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]

    run = wait_for_state(hr_admin_client, f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}", "completed", timeout=10)
    assert run["state"] == "completed"

    stubs_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs")
    assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
    stubs = stubs_resp.json()["items"]
    assert len(stubs) == 1

    stub = stubs[0]
    assert stub["employee_id"] == seed_single_employee["id"]
    assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
    assert stub["net_pay"] == stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"]

tests.name: test_payroll_run_state_transition_idle_to_running_to_completed
preconditions: system is in idle state (no run for the period)
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs — observe initial state is queued or running (not completed yet)
poll /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} repeatedly until state transitions through running to completed
expected: state sequence satisfies: (queued|running) -> completed
state never goes backwards (e.g. completed -> running is absent)
final state is completed
HTTP 200 on each poll with valid state field
trace: STC: idle -> running -> completed transition path
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_state_transition_idle_to_running_to_completed(
    hr_admin_client, seed_active_employees, current_month_period, unique_idem_key
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        "/api/payroll-runs",
        json={"period": current_month_period},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": unique_idem_key},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]
    initial_state = resp.json()["state"]
    assert initial_state in ("queued", "running", "completed")

    observed_states = collect_state_transitions(
        hr_admin_client, f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        terminal_states={"completed", "failed"}, timeout=15
    )

    valid_forward = {"queued", "running", "completed"}
    assert all(s in valid_forward for s in observed_states)
    assert observed_states[-1] == "completed"
    # no backward transition: completed must not be followed by running/queued
    for i in range(len(observed_states) - 1):
        assert not (observed_states[i] == "completed" and
                    observed_states[i + 1] in ("queued", "running"))
